inction in
respect of horoscopy and horary astrology. He states that for
horoscopy there should not be any difference in the lordship of
the decanates and that should belong to the lords of the 1st.,
5th and 9th houses, while for the purpose of queries the lords are
to be taken as those of the 1st, 12th and 11th houses. 1 am
inclined to believe that this distinction is very helpful in the case
of horary astrology. It is to be borne in mind that every planet
is considered to be strong while posited in his own decanate.
These decanates have their own names, forms, characteristic
qualities and utilities. The names of the decanates are the follow-
ing (/) Sarpa or serpent, (ii) Nigala or fetters, (Hi) Ayudha
or weapon, (iv) Paksin or bird, (v) Dahana or fiery, (vi) Jala or
watery, (vu) Saumya or benefic. and (viii) Vimisra or mixed.
There is also sex-difference among them. Some of them are
also called Catuspad or quadruped. The following arc termed
